_meta.name: audit_probe
_meta.note: Tlumaczenie 1000 wpisow z audit probe: etykiety filtrow ekwipunku, generator reguł pokerowych (Cactus Bill), minigra Spittleships, drumla, strzelanie do klozetu, dialogi towarzysza-kokosa, zepsuta (naprzemienna wielkość liter, celowo zachowana) transmisja zlego klauna, minigra diamondhead, kalambury kaktusa-towarzysza, linie przywolania barmana, skargi na opoznienie pociagu, linie 'kon/pardner niesie cie do domu'. WAZNE: indeksy odpowiadajace kluczom buckets.reference_*/ripple_*/rumbles (ok. 640 wpisow, w tym cale bloki 'STATS AND STUFF', dokumentacja konsoli debug, lista komend skryptowych, notatki o stylu wypowiedzi postaci, podziekowania) to WEWNETRZNA dokumentacja deweloperska nigdy nie pokazywana graczom - zostawione bez zmian (kopia EN=PL), nie tlumaczone. Dwie prawdziwe kwestie gracza ukryte w tym bloku (buckets.roberto_cultistshouts) zostaly przetlumaczone normalnie.

The debug console is an in-game \"drop-d.pl: The debug console is an in-game "drop-down" style command line for West of Loathing. It can:

- show you some logging info from the g.pl: - show you some logging info from the game

- perform some internal functions for t.pl: - perform some internal functions for the game

To bring up the debug console, press you.pl: To bring up the debug console, press your keyboard's '~' key. Press '~' again to close the console. The game will pause while the console is open.

Logging - If something goes wrong in the.pl: Logging - If something goes wrong in the internals of the game engine, or is just an interesting event, the game will log that to the debug console. Some things that are logged:

- loading game data from the data.json.pl: - loading game data from the data.json file

- history and output from debug command.pl: - history and output from debug commands

- /reload - download and then load up t.pl: - /reload - download and then load up the text data (data.json) from the server

- /reload all - /reload, but also downl.pl: - /reload all - /reload, but also download all of the sprites

- /reload local - don't download data.j.pl: - /reload local - don't download data.json, but reload the file from disk

- /flags - print out all of the current.pl: - /flags - print out all of the current flags

- /flag <name> - print out the value of.pl: - /flag <name> - print out the value of the flag called <name>

- /load - load the last save for the cu.pl: - /load - load the last save for the current character

- /load <name> - load the last save for.pl: - /load <name> - load the last save for the named character

- /cls - clear all messages from the lo.pl: - /cls - clear all messages from the log

- /commands - print out a list of known.pl: - /commands - print out a list of known script commands. Not necessarily all supported by the debug console, see below

- /madlib <string>,<count> - print out.pl: - /madlib <string>,<count> - print out <string>, madlibbed, <count> times. If <count> isn't there, it default to 10.

Script Commands - Script commands fall i.pl: Script Commands - Script commands fall into three categories. Normal commands are just like lines of script; expressions calculate the value of some math and/or logic expression; conditions tell you if using a condition in an 'if' command in a script would run the 'if' block or the 'else' block.

Normal commands: These can be run by ent.pl: Normal commands: These can be run by entering the command name, open parent, the arguments separated by commas, and a close paren.

You could also leave the parens out.pl: You could also leave the parens out

Note that some commands don't really wor.pl: Note that some commands don't really work here - if, else, goto, and others just don't make sense without a whole script surrounding them.

Expressions: The value of some expressio.pl: Expressions: The value of some expression can be gotten by entering '=' and then the expression. For example,

will cause the console to print out the.pl: will cause the console to print out the value of the flag 'cactuspricks'

Conditions: A condition can be checked b.pl: Conditions: A condition can be checked by entering '?' and then the condition, like

Checking a condition will always result.pl: Checking a condition will always result in either "True" or "False". Most conditions could also be evaluated with '=', in which case a number would be output instead.
